topic_text: When Any event starts from Google Calendar, do Send blink event using blink(1). 15 mins before an event in your Google Calendar make your blink(1) shine. Stop missing meetings or appointments! Get a visual reminder with this Recipe: your blink(1) USB light will shine before your scheduled events.

When Every day of the week at from Date & Time, do Set vacation mode using GE Appliances GeoSpring. Put your water heater in vacation mode every weekend. It's quitin' time! Save money at your business or vacation home by putting your water heater in vacation mode when no one is using hot water.

When Every day of the week at from Date & Time, do Set operating mode using GE Appliances GeoSpring. Every Monday morning put the water heater in Hybrid mode.. Pair this recipe with an accompanying recipe that previously put the water heater into vacation modeUse both to manage your savings.

When Every day of the week at from Date & Time, do Set temperature using GE Appliances GeoSpring. Time of Use rates... CoastingThis is the second of 3 Applets for Weekday Time of Use rate savings with the water heater The first one charged up the water heater with extra heat in a low rate time This one lets it coast across the high rate period using minimal energy The third Applet will let it recharge up to normal temperature with low cost energy again Time periods are for weekdays in PG&E utility from May 1- Oct 31.

When Every day of the week at from Date & Time, do Set temperature using GE Appliances GeoSpring. During weekend Off-Peak rate periods, Recharge water heater temperature. This is the second of two recipes to save money by charging up the water heater in off-peak rate periods (the other recipe lets it coast across the peak rate period at a lower set point relying more on stored hot water energy).

When Every day of the week at from Date & Time, do Set temperature using GE Appliances GeoSpring. Winter Charge-Up off-peak. Recipe 1 of 2Winter Charge-Up off-peak  Before peak-rate time each day, set water heater temperature up to 130ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ°F  This is Recipe 1 of 2 Recipe 2 is called Winter Coasting on-peak.

When Every day of the week at from Date & Time, do Set temperature using GE Appliances GeoSpring. Winter Coasting on-peak. Recipe 2 of 2Winter Coasting on-peak  At on-peak rate time each day, set water heater temperature down to 110ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ°F  This is Recipe 2 of 2 Recipe 1 is called Winter Charge-Up off-peak.

Hello fellasThis Applet works in combo with "Set thermostat to a specific schedule when Airbnb host arrives"You just have to create an event on your google calendar and name it 'Netatmo - Thermostat: Away'set the date on day of any guest check-out and the thermostat will go into away modeHope you will find usefulPlease favThanks, caiulogic

When Any event starts from Google Calendar, do Change color using Philips Hue. If my calendar says I'm "busy", make the Hue light at my desk "red". This is how I tell my colleagues that I'm not to be disturbed at the moment.

When Event from search starts from Google Calendar, do Switch schedule using Netatmo Thermostat. Set thermostat schedule when Airbnb guest arrives. Hello peopleI am an airbnb host and this recipe is helping me saving a lot of moneyIt sets my Netatmo Thermostat to a specific schedule whenever it finds a specific query in the name of any Google Calendar event.Remember to name your events: "Netatmo thermostat: Guest IN"This recipe will be followed by another one that sets the thermostat to "Away" when your guest will check-out.Hope you will find it usefulPlease press on that nice heart.Thanks,Caiulogic

When Every day of the week at from Date & Time, do Set temperature using GE Appliances GeoSpring. Time of Use Rates... Charge UpThis is the first of 3 Applets for saving money with Time of Use Rates.First Applet CHARGES up the water heater temperature using cheap electricity.Second Applet "COASTS" the water heater across the high rate period with minimal energy.Third Applet RECHARGES water heater to medium temperature after rates go back down  Use all three Applets together for full control Time periods are for PG&E utility TOU rate E-6 from May 1 to Oct 31.

When Every day of the week at from Date & Time, do Set temperature using GE Appliances GeoSpring. Time of Use Rates Recharge. This is the 3rd of 3 recipes for saving money with a water heater with Time of Use utility rates The first one charged up the water heater temperature with low cost energy The second one coasted across the high rate period with stored energy This third one recharges the water heater to a normal temperature as soon as rates drop to their low level.

When Event from search starts from Google Calendar, do Set temperature using Nest Thermostat. If event from search for 'Off' starts on Google Calendar, then set temperature to __ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ°. I work a 5 week shift rotation so Nest's built in schedule planner is not sufficient but Google Calendar enables me to plan a schedule over several weeks, even months.1Create a new calendar on Google Calendar.2Plan your schedule by creating an event (The event can't be 0 minutes long) but be sure to use either the event name "On" or "Off" to correspond with the Applet search.3Specify your desired temperature below and save.I have also published the Applet of the alternate event so be sure to add that to your Applet's tooYou can also be adventurous and add more events with different temperatures but be sure to use a different name for each event to correspond with your different AppletsThis is mine: https://www.google.com/calendar/embed?src=95vqhk979k01o3iqu3isun9aio%40group.calendar.google.com&ctz=Europe/London

When Event from search starts from Google Calendar, do Set temperature using Nest Thermostat. If event from search for 'On' starts on Google Calendar, then set temperature to __ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ°. I work a 5 week shift rotation so Nest's built in schedule planner is not sufficient but Google Calendar enables me to plan a schedule over several weeks, even months.1Create a new calendar on Google Calendar.2Plan your schedule by creating an event (The event can't be 0 minutes long) but be sure to use either the event name "On" or "Off" to correspond with the Applet search.3Specify your desired temperature below and save.I have also published the Applet of the alternate event so be sure to add that to your Applet's tooYou can also be adventurous and add more events with different temperatures but be sure to use a different name for each event to correspond with your different AppletsThis is mine: https://www.google.com/calendar/embed?src=95vqhk979k01o3iqu3isun9aio%40group.calendar.google.com&ctz=Europe/London
